description: Preparatory refactoring of the legacy code/ directory to fix cross-platform blockers, reduce GUI coupling, and replace Win32-specific APIs before migrating to the new src/ modular layout.

userStories.title: Fix Include Case Sensitivity
userStories.description: Normalize all #include directives so the quoted filename matches the actual filename on disk. Windows is case-insensitive; Linux is not — mismatches cause build failures on Linux.
acceptanceCriteria: Every #include "..." directive in code/ uses exactly the same casing as the file on disk
A verification pass (grep + ls comparison) confirms zero case mismatches
Only .h and .cpp files in code/ are modified
No behavioral changes — refactoring only

userStories.title: Extract Utility Functions from gdioutput
userStories.description: Move non-GUI utility functions (widen, narrow, toUTF8, fromUTF8) out of gdioutput.h/cpp to meos_util.h/cpp so domain files can drop the GUI dependency.
acceptanceCriteria: widen(), narrow(), toUTF8(), fromUTF8() are defined in meos_util.h/cpp (or a new string_util.h/cpp)
gdioutput.h retains thin wrappers, using declarations, or re-includes so existing callers don't break
Domain files that only needed these string utilities no longer directly include gdioutput.h
No behavioral changes — refactoring only

userStories.title: Replace Win32-Specific String Functions in Domain Code
userStories.description: Replace Windows-only string functions (_wtoi, sprintf_s, swprintf_s, _itow_s) with standard C++ equivalents in domain files.
acceptanceCriteria: No direct calls to _wtoi, sprintf_s, swprintf_s, or _itow_s in domain .cpp/.h files (oEvent*, oRunner, oClass, oClub, oCourse, oControl, oCard, oTeam, oDataContainer, oBase, oFreePunch, and similar domain files)
Replacements use standard C++ (<cstdlib>, <string>, <cwchar>): _wtoi -> std::stoi or wcstol; sprintf_s -> snprintf; swprintf_s -> swprintf; _itow_s -> std::to_wstring or swprintf
UI files (Tab*, gdioutput) are NOT modified
No behavioral changes — refactoring only
userStories.notes: Replaced _wtoi, sprintf_s, swprintf_s, _itow_s with std::stoi, snprintf, swprintf in all domain files.

userStories.title: Replace Win32 Types with Standard Types in Domain Code
userStories.description: Replace Win32-specific type aliases (DWORD, BOOL) with standard C++ types in domain code.
acceptanceCriteria: No DWORD usage in domain .cpp/.h files — replaced with uint32_t (or unsigned int/int depending on context)
No Win32 BOOL usage in domain .cpp/.h files — replaced with bool (check for TRUE/FALSE comparisons)
#include <cstdint> added where needed for uint32_t
UI files (Tab*, gdioutput) are NOT modified
No behavioral changes — refactoring only

userStories.title: Normalize Path Separators in Domain Code
userStories.description: Replace hardcoded backslash path separators with cross-platform alternatives using std::filesystem::path in domain code.
acceptanceCriteria: Domain files use std::filesystem::path or portable path construction instead of hardcoded backslash separators
Hardcoded backslash path separators (non-escape-sequence uses) are replaced in domain files
#include <filesystem> added where needed
Escape sequences in strings (e.g. \n, \t) are NOT modified — only path separators
UI files (Tab*, gdioutput) are NOT modified
No behavioral changes — refactoring only

userStories.title: Decouple oEvent from Tab UI Classes
userStories.description: Remove oEvent.cpp's direct coupling to Tab UI classes (TabList, TabAuto, TabSI) by replacing direct calls with std::function callbacks.
acceptanceCriteria: oEvent.cpp no longer includes TabList.h, TabAuto.h, or TabSI.h
TabList::baseButtons() call in oEvent replaced with a std::function callback
TabAuto::tabAutoKillMachines() call in oEvent replaced with a std::function callback
TabSI::getSI().setSubSecondMode() call in oEvent replaced with a std::function callback
Tab classes register their callbacks during application initialization (e.g. in main() or startup code)
Callbacks stored as std::function members in oEvent
No behavioral changes — refactoring only
